notes: Industry-standard cut-rifled match blank from 416R stainless. Bartlein is the highest-volume cut-rifled maker in the US and is ubiquitous on PRS / F-class / NRA Long Range rifles. Available in 5R and conventional rifling. Wisconsin-based.

Single-point cut-rifled blanks; long-time benchrest and high-power favorite. 416R stainless. Wisconsin-based, owned by John Krieger. Known for very tight bore tolerances and conventional or 4-groove canted-land rifling.
Cut-rifled 416R stainless match blanks. Founded by former Krieger employees; Wisconsin-based. Strong reputation in PRS and F-class circles for accuracy and consistency.

Carbon-fiber-wrapped stainless match barrel; Sendero contour optimized for hunting / tactical weight. The carbon wrap dramatically reduces weight versus an equivalent steel contour while maintaining stiffness and adding heat dissipation.
Heavier-profile carbon-wrapped barrel for precision rifle / PRS work where added rigidity outweighs weight savings. Same carbon-wrap technology as Sendero Light.
Short-range / semi-auto-optimized carbon-wrapped contour. Common on AR-10 / AR-15 precision builds.
Steel-only Proof Research barrel for buyers who want their cut-rifled match accuracy without the carbon wrap. Same chambering and rifling process as the carbon-wrapped line.
Cut-rifled 416R stainless match blanks. Based in Caldwell, Idaho. Widely used in PRS and tactical rifle builds; reputation built on consistent bore dimensions and quick-shipping cycles.

German-made polygonal-rifled stainless match barrel; LW50 alloy is Lothar Walther's proprietary chrome-moly stainless. Widely used in ISSF smallbore, Olympic-style air rifle, and benchrest. Also offered in conventional rifling.
Lower-cost chrome-moly hammer-forged barrel from Lothar Walther's Germany works. Common OEM choice for European hunting rifles.
Button-rifled 416R stainless match blank; Shilen's flagship grade. Texas-based maker with deep benchrest history dating to the 1970s. Available in conventional and ratchet (5R-style) rifling.
Button-rifled chrome-moly steel barrel for hunting and varmint rifles. Less expensive than Select Match stainless but same rifling process.
Cut-rifled 416R stainless match blanks. Arlington, Washington-based; family-run since 1990s. Common in F-class and PRS builds.
Button-rifled 416R stainless match barrel. Brookings, Oregon-based. Pac-Nor pioneered hand-lapping of button-rifled barrels and is a long-time custom-rifle staple.
Button-rifled 4140 chrome-moly steel for hunting and tactical rifles. Same rifling and lapping process as Super Match stainless.
Cut-rifled 416R stainless match blanks. Albany, Oregon-based. Strong following in benchrest and PRS; known for very straight bores and tight tolerances.
Cut-rifled 416R stainless match blanks. Plains, Montana-based. Lilja makes both conventional and 3-groove rifling and is a common choice for ELR and benchrest. Founded by Dan Lilja.

Match-grade 416R stainless core with carbon-fiber wrap. Christensen pioneered carbon-wrapped barrels in the late 1990s. Sold both as factory-rifle barrels and as drop-in / pre-fit blanks for Remington 700 and other actions.

Button-rifled 416R stainless match barrel. Kalispell, Montana-based. McGowen offers both blank and pre-fit (Savage / Remington / Tikka shouldered) configurations and is popular in mid-budget precision builds.
Button-rifled 416R stainless. Faxon is the largest AR-platform precision barrel supplier in the US; also offers bolt-action match blanks. Cincinnati, Ohio-based.
Cut-rifled 416R stainless match blanks. Founded by former Bartlein machinists. Known for fast lead-times and tight tolerances; growing reputation in PRS circles.
UK-based cut-rifled barrel maker (Newcastleton, Scotland). Founded by Geoff Kolbe. Highly regarded in F-class and Palma circles internationally; one of the few European cut-rifled match makers.
New Zealand-based cut-rifled match barrel maker. 416R stainless. Strong following in the Australasian F-class and benchrest community; growing US distribution.
Cut-rifled 416R stainless match blanks. Run by Frank Green (formerly of Bartlein); Wisconsin-based. Smaller-volume custom shop with a reputation for handpicked tolerances.

Button-rifled and air-gauged 416R stainless. Charleston, West Virginia. One of the oldest custom barrel makers in the US (founded 1947); the XX grade is air-gauged for tighter tolerances. Also offered in chrome-moly.
Button-rifled, air-gauged 4140 chrome-moly. Same XX premium grading as the stainless line.
E.R. Shaw button-rifled 416R stainless match barrel. Bridgeville, Pennsylvania-based, family-owned since 1915. Common OEM choice for budget-friendly precision builds and pre-fit barrels.
Match-grade stainless core with carbon-fiber wrap. Newer carbon-wrap entrant (founded 2018) competing directly with Proof Research. Known for proprietary heat-management lay-up.
